title: Fix compilers seeing empty input due to file system races
body:
Hi there!

## Problem

I noticed that in some very specific conditions, Verilator generates C++ code that fails with the following error message:
```
tb.o: in function `main':
undefined reference to `Vtop::Vtop(char const*)'
undefined reference to `Vtop::~Vtop()'
collect2: error: ld returned 1 exit status
```

## Source code

The sources are extremely simple:
tb.cc:
```
#include "Vtop.h"
#include "verilated.h"

typedef Vtop Module;

int main(int argc, char **argv, char **env) {

  Verilated::commandArgs(argc, argv);
  Verilated::traceEverOn(VM_TRACE);

  Module *my_module = new Module;
  delete my_module;
  exit(0);
}
```

top.sv:
```
module top(in_data, out_data);
  input in_data;
  wire in_data;
  output [1503:0] out_data;
  wire [1503:0] out_data;
  assign out_data[0] = in_data;
endmodule
```

## Command

The following command, under specific path conditions that are hard to identify, gives the error mentioned above.
```
verilator --cc --exe --build /some/path/tb.cc /some/path/top.sv -CFLAGS '-I/som/other/path/include -I/some/path -g' --Mdir /some/path/obj_dir
```

## Observation about sensitivity

Surprisingly, if I modify any path, for example an include path or Mdir path, then the error disappears.

## obj_dir in both cases

I provide the obj_dir generated by the command above, and the one generated by the same command where I replaced obj_dir with obj_dir_nobug (in that case, the compilation succeeds).

[obj_dir.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13415283/obj_dir.zip)
[obj_dir_nobug.zip](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13415296/obj_dir_nobug.zip)

The most obvious difference visible in `obj_dir.zip` is that `Vtop__ALL.d` is only containing `Vtop__ALL.o: Vtop__ALL.cpp`.

## Debug logs

Here are also the `--debug` logs from Verilator, although they do not provide much more information.

[cmd.log](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13415365/cmd.log)
[cmd_nobug.log](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/13415366/cmd_nobug.log)

## Reproducibility

I've been trying to reproduce this in a Docker container but it is still hard to reproduce as I do not yet clearly understand the causes. On the machine, with the same file and paths, the error always occurs. I hope you will have some ideas.

Thanks!
Flavien
